purpose: Public Exafuse proof-map signals used to strengthen Manish Sharma Lab without duplicating company service pages or treating process-monitoring content as certification.

case_studies.title: Duisburg Bridge Components with LMD
public_signals: Knoten 10: 219 hours and about 8 km of robot travel
more than one million melt-pool images

case_studies.title: Forging Hammer Repair and Coating with LMD
public_signals: hardness is not enough by itself

case_studies.title: 130 mm Bombenbohrer Build-and-Coat Workflow
public_signals: LMD fabrication plus LMD functional coating
wear-resistant anti-magnetic coating from tungsten-carbide-containing alloy

knowledge_articles.title: LMD buyer guide
public_signals: repair, cladding, modification and hybrid routes

knowledge_articles.title: LMD vs SLM selection
public_signals: LMD fit: large parts, repair, local feature addition and cladding
SLM / LPBF fit: compact complex geometry, fine detail and internal channels
process choice depends on geometry, purpose, post-processing and inspection

knowledge_articles.title: Large-part LMD productivity
public_signals: Titan build envelope 2 x 1 x 2 m
robotic rotary-table support up to 1,000 kg
large LMD depends on bead width, overlap, heat management, machining allowance, fixtures and inspection

knowledge_articles.title: Monitoring and control
public_signals: monitoring supports process awareness but does not replace inspection or qualification

knowledge_articles.title: BreitBahnDED wide-bead research
public_signals: conventional LMD track context around 1-4 mm
research exploration around 5 mm and potentially 10 mm tracks
30-50% time-saving potential as a research goal

knowledge_articles.title: 2024 year in powder
public_signals: more than 1,850 kg of LMD material in 2024
around 250 kg across nickel, wear-resistant, copper and specialty steel routes

knowledge_articles.title: Neural image processing
public_signals: model outputs require process-context and inspection validation

claim_boundary: These are public Exafuse proof signals. They support public technical context and buyer education, not engineering approval, certification, guaranteed outcomes, or confidential project details.
